description: In early 2016, IBM initiated a massive workforce reduction program, internally termed 'Project Chrome', as part of a pivot toward cloud and AI services. The move sparked significant public backlash due to reports that long-term employees were being forced to train their offshore replacements before being laid off, leading to a heated debate on corporate loyalty versus digital transformation.

key_events.description: Reports emerge detailing the 'train your replacement' requirement for legacy US staff.
Internal IBM memos leaked to ProPublica detailing the scale of the restructuring.
Tech labor unions and media commentators label the restructuring as 'age discrimination'.
IBM CEO Ginni Rometty defends the restructuring as necessary for survival in the cloud era.

calibration_notes: This scenario calibrates public perception of corporate restructuring vs. ethical obligation to legacy employees, providing a clear trajectory from initial corporate support to widespread public moral opposition.
